text.title: microservices-observability Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for microservices-observability. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Every inbound HTTP request MUST open a trace span and every outbound HTTP call or message bus publish MUST propagate the traceparent header. A service that does not propagate traceparent breaks the trace for all downstream services.
rule.rationale: Without end-to-end trace propagation, a distributed trace is a collection of disconnected local traces. The value of distributed tracing is the cross-service correlation, which requires every service to propagate the context.

rule.statement: Every event class MUST be registered in a schema registry (Apicurio, Confluent Schema Registry, Buf Schema Registry) and compatibility checks MUST be enforced as a CI gate before any producer is deployed.
rule.rationale: Event payloads are public APIs once consumed by another service. A breaking change (field rename, type change, required field added) crashes consumers silently at runtime. The registry's compatibility check is the only automated gate that catches this before production.
